input: That's not covered. Nothing I can do.
output: This particular case falls outside our coverage, so let me dig into what alternatives we might have before we call it a dead end.

input: You broke it, not us.
output: Happy to help get to the bottom of this. Could you walk me through what happened so we can work out the best next step?

input: The discount expired. Not our problem.
output: That offer has closed, but there are a few active promotions I can check against your order right now if you'd like.

input: Your ticket is in the queue. Just wait.
output: Your request is being worked through in order of priority. I'll flag it internally to make sure nothing slips through the cracks.

input: We don't do partial refunds.
output: A partial refund isn't on the table here, though store credit is something I can apply right now if that would help.

input: I have no idea why it's not working.
output: This one's stumping me too, so I'd rather hand you to someone on our technical side who can give it a proper look.

input: System's down. Come back later.
output: We're dealing with an outage right now and the team is actively on it. Apologies for the disruption — things should be back up shortly.

input: You entered the wrong address. That's on you.
output: Shipping to the wrong address is a stressful situation. Let me see whether the carrier can redirect it before it gets too far along.

input: We won't make exceptions.
output: Exceptions aren't something we can grant automatically, but your situation sounds worth escalating. Give me a moment to check with my supervisor.

input: Read our return policy. It's all there.
output: The policy covers this, and I'm glad to pull out the relevant section and explain exactly how it applies to your order.

input: That plan doesn't include this. Upgrade.
output: That feature lives on a higher tier, so it'd require a plan change. Want me to run you through what the upgrade covers before you decide?

input: Already answered this. Check your email.
output: We did send a reply on this — if it hasn't come through or left things unclear, I'm right here and happy to go over it again.

input: Your account is locked. Not much we can do right now.
output: Getting locked out is never fun. Once I verify a couple of details on my end, I can get that access restored for you.

input: Shipping is delayed everywhere. Welcome to the world.
output: Delays have been hitting carriers hard industry-wide, and I genuinely appreciate your patience. Your order is still moving and I can share the latest tracking.

input: That's the price. Take it or leave it.
output: Pricing is set, though I can check whether any discounts or bundles apply to your account that might bring the cost down.

input: Not my team's issue. Someone else handles that.
output: You need a different team for this one. I'll transfer you now and pass along everything we've discussed so you won't have to start over.

input: We can't override the system.
output: Automated restrictions do block that action, but a manual review request is something I can submit on your behalf right now.

input: Just reinstall the app.
output: A clean reinstall is worth trying and fixes this more often than not. If the problem persists after that, come back and we'll take a harder look.

input: You're past the return window. Too bad.
output: The return period for this order has closed, though I'd still like to review the circumstances before giving you a final answer.

input: We sent you an email about this already.
output: An update was sent out — if it didn't land in your inbox or raised more questions, I'm available to clear things up right now.

input: That's a known bug. Engineering is working on it.
output: Our team has confirmed this bug and a fix is in progress. I'll get your contact added to the resolution notification so you hear the moment it's live.

input: Don't use that browser then.
output: There's a known compatibility issue with that browser on our end. Chrome or Firefox should get you up and running straight away.

input: Can't process that without manager approval. They're busy.
output: This does need sign-off from a manager, and I've already flagged it as time-sensitive. I'll reach out to you directly as soon as I have a response.

input: Your file is too big. Compress it.
output: The upload limit is being hit here. Compressing the file or breaking it into smaller chunks will sort it — just say the word if you'd like a hand with that.

input: We don't support that country.
output: Your region isn't in our service area yet, though expansion is ongoing. I can add you to our early-access list so you're notified as soon as it opens up.

input: The promo code is wrong. Check it again.
output: The code isn't applying on our end. Share it with me and I'll verify whether it's still valid and what might be causing the issue.

input: We process payments in batches. It takes time.
output: Payments run through in scheduled batches, which can mean a brief delay before it shows on your statement. If it hasn't appeared within 24 hours, please do get back to us.

input: That's just how the product works.
output: That behavior is intentional on the design side, though I understand it's not ideal. There's a workaround a lot of users find useful — want me to show you?

input: You're asking the wrong department.
output: That sits with a different team. I'll connect you now and make sure they have the full picture before you get on.

input: We don't offer phone support. Use the chat.
output: Phone support isn't available for this type of issue, but our chat team handles it well and response times are usually very quick.

input: That account was deleted. It's gone.
output: The account has been removed from our system. Let me check on our end whether anything is recoverable, or I can help you get a new one set up smoothly.

input: You didn't confirm your email. That's why it's not working.
output: The confirmation step looks like it was skipped — not a problem at all. I'll resend the link now and stick around until you're fully verified.

input: We can only do one exchange per order.
output: One exchange is the limit per order, but a store credit could give you more room to work with. Want me to see if that applies here?

input: Your payment failed. Use a different card.
output: The payment didn't clear this time, which can happen for a few different reasons. Trying another card or method usually does it — let me know if it still won't go through.

input: That's not a feature we're adding.
output: That isn't something we have planned right now, but feedback like yours does shape the roadmap. I'll make sure this gets passed to the product team.

input: We can't reverse that transaction.
output: A direct reversal isn't available for this transaction type, but filing a dispute is an option I can initiate for you right now along with what to expect from the process.

input: Our SLA is 48 hours. You've only waited 20.
output: You're still within our response window, and your case hasn't been forgotten. If you haven't heard back by the 48-hour mark, reach out and I'll chase it down personally.

input: That error means your internet is bad.
output: That error code can point to a few things, and connectivity is one of them. Let's rule out the easy stuff first before assuming the worst.

input: We don't have that information on file.
output: We're not showing that on our end. If you can send over the relevant details, I'll get the record updated straight away.

input: Not sure why you're confused. It's pretty clear.
output: Totally fair if that didn't land clearly — let me try explaining it a different way and please jump in if anything still doesn't click.
